checklist_summary.note: batch1=30 (workflows/schema/docs) + batch2=16 (test code). Lite probes all PASS; VC-20 (devflow-implement.yml 'sync with devflow.yml literal list' comment) PASS — devflow.yml line 544 carries the literal TOOLS list; VC-21 (section 14/17 cross-refs) PASS — bidirectional and consistent with sibling config-table rows; behavioral claims corroborated by the 116 passing #313 assertions + Phase-3 agents.

phase3_findings.description: empty BASE_REF leaves config_source='absent' (should be 'degraded') — OAuth-guard message misdirection; fail-closed, near-unreachable
config_source producer branches (baseprovision step) untested — only the OAuth-guard consumer was covered
empty-string auth has no dedicated matrix row (transitively covered)
AC8 dead 'model' input removal has no explicit pin
docs/cloud-setup.md 'exported unfiltered' — env-map keys are in fact charset-validated (fail-loud); wording defensible in intended sense (no semantic denylist), not a documented_falsehood
env-map key validation is charset-only; a valid-charset-but-dangerous name (PATH, GITHUB_TOKEN) would export job-wide. Trusted (base-ref/default-branch) input; documented-only posture is defensible

runner MODEL fallback comment frames only the bootstrap case; provider-active+no-model also lets head config pick the model NAME (endpoint/auth/key stay base-trusted; flag-injection guard constrains). Narrow, human-merge advisory

fix_decisions.evidence: The word 'unfiltered' is accurate in its intended sense — there is no SEMANTIC denylist of well-formed dangerous names; the very next doc clause warns 'do not name a runtime-sensitive variable here (PATH, GITHUB_TOKEN, ...)'. comment-analyzer explicitly declined to grade it a documented_falsehood. Not a self-contradicting diff.

fix_decisions.claim_text: add a runtime denylist for dangerous env-map key names
fix_decisions.evidence: The env map is read only from maintainer/base-ref-controlled TRUSTED config (base-ref for the runner; trusted default-branch checkout for the command workflows). The final-pass reviewer graded it Suggestion and stated 'the current state is safe under the trusted-config assumption' and 'the documented-only posture is a defensible choice'. Adding a denylist is an optional design decision the author deliberately made (documented in cloud-setup.md); not applied per receiving-code-review (don't blindly implement an optional hardening on trusted input).

fix_decisions.claim_text: MODEL fallback comment should note the provider-active+no-model case hands the model NAME to head config
fix_decisions.evidence: The comment already states the fallback fires 'whenever the base-resolved model is empty — from ANY cause' (not only bootstrap), so it is not false. Security impact is narrow: only the model NAME (not endpoint/auth/key, all base-trusted) can be head-selected, and only when base config names a provider but sets no model anywhere (unusual); the flag-injection guard constrains the value and a human still merges. Suggestion-tier advisory; comment is accurate.

stale_prose_check.rows: 0 STALE; 2 UNRESOLVABLE R3 (recognition-only count claims in pre-existing test comments, informational, never gate). Post-fix on committed HEAD: #434 self-scan PASS (no stale counted-prose in the fix additions; the 'five outcomes' comment was reworded drift-proof before commit).

phase_2.note: lite probes + orchestrator-direct verification; no verifier subagents dispatched (cheap-path)

step_2_5.note: no external-tool single-source claims requiring web verification

fix_delta_gate.note: blinded subagent verified all 6 config_source fixtures live, confirmed empty-BASE_REF assertion is genuine red-before-green, operand-contract honored (degraded in the ok/absent/degraded consumer set). 2 cosmetic Suggestions (pre-existing OAuth-remedy text imprecision on near-unreachable empty-ref path; my test comment says 'disjunct' for an and-conjunction) — advisory per gate routing.

shadow.trigger: early (engine_self_modifying, after iteration 1 which routed to fixes)

phase3_findings.description: env-map key guard `jq -e ... >/dev/null 2>&1` masks jq's own error rc (fail-open direction if .env were ever non-object); NOT reachable today (resolver guarantees .env object); latent-coupling defense-in-depth across the 3 byte-identical inject blocks

single-sourced cargs SECURITY comment's threat-provenance parenthetical (bootstrap fallback / caller-effort-input / PR-head) is runner-specific but byte-copied into devflow.yml/devflow-implement.yml; operative claim ('refuse flag-smuggling values') correct in all 3; NOT a documented_falsehood
corroborates iter-1 CA-1: cloud-setup.md 'exported unfiltered' understates the charset validation (defensible in intended sense)

4 test-completeness nits (crit 2-3): <<-carrying env-key test exit-code-only; effort_supported non-bool tests string not number 1; 3 degraded producer paths assert value not branch (acceptable, empty-ref IS discriminated); runner degraded/absent provider-path composition covered only transitively

shadow.park_calibration_gate: clean: no parked finding matched a mis-grade shape — CA-1 is a precision nit on a defensible statement (next clause warns of dangerous names); RCR-1 env-map name-denylist is an optional hardening on TRUSTED (base-ref/default-branch) input, not an under-specified-vs-AC guard, and the guard does catch the malformed-KEY inputs it was added for; RCR-2 comment already says 'from ANY cause'; shadow-SFH-NEW is not-reachable latent coupling (the guard's own added-for inputs — malformed keys — ARE caught), graded LOW by its own author.
shadow.over_grade_calibration_gate: clean: no promote-path finding flagged (no outcome-2 promotion — every new shadow finding is Suggestion/LOW).
shadow.notes: Early shadow ran a full-coverage blinded fan-out over the post-fix HEAD 4b98009a and AGREED (shadow_verdict APPROVE, no new Critical/Important). All new findings Suggestion/LOW; final-pass's config.example.json suggestion is CONTRARY to AC2 (which mandates leaving it untouched) → non-finding. Loop converged: this early shadow serves as the convergence audit — it covered the exact final post-fix HEAD with full coverage and agreement, so a separate iteration 2 (which would apply zero code fixes, all residuals being advisory Suggestions) plus a redundant convergence-time shadow over the unchanged HEAD would only re-confirm this result. Post-shadow edit gate: HEAD unchanged since the shadow (still 4b98009a) — no unreviewed final edit.
